warnings: D6 (engines.bun/node): These are metadata-only fields. Mend MUST NOT treat them as dependency blocks or generate phantom library entries for 'bun' or 'node'. They should round-trip into project_metadata only. Failure mode: parser mistakes engines values for dep versions, generating false-positive deps named 'bun' and 'node'.

D7 (private: true): Application-project marker. Has no effect on the dependency tree shape. Mend MUST still detect and report all deps regardless of this field. Failure mode: parser short-circuits on private:true and returns an empty tree (treating it as 'nothing to publish, skip scanning').

D8 (bundleDependencies: ['is-odd']): is-odd is listed for tar-bundling on publish, but it remains a normal resolved dependency in the lockfile. Mend MUST include is-odd in the tree. Failure mode: parser treats bundleDependencies as an exclusion list and drops is-odd, causing a false negative. Additionally, is-number (transitive via is-odd) would also be lost.

D9 (workspaces.nohoist): Bun parses but currently ignores the nohoist array at install time. The workspaces field uses the OBJECT form ({packages, nohoist}) rather than the ARRAY shorthand. Failure mode (parser): parser expects workspaces to be an array and crashes on the object form, breaking tree extraction for the entire project. Failure mode (behavior): Mend propagates nohoist semantics and hoists packages differently than expected.

workspaces OBJECT form: This probe uses { 'packages': ['packages/*'], 'nohoist': [...] } instead of the simpler string-array shorthand. A parser that expects only the array form will either crash or ignore the workspaces declaration entirely.

Bun is NOT in the Mend install-tool list. Detection is lockfile-driven only (bun.lock JSONC parsing). scanSettings.versioning cannot pin a Bun toolchain. No .whitesource file is emitted with this probe.

All four metadata fields (engines, private, bundleDependencies, workspaces.nohoist) appear ONLY in package.json, not in bun.lock. Mend must cross-read package.json for project_metadata while using bun.lock for dependency resolution.

The packages/ directory exists but contains no workspace members. This is valid — the workspaces glob 'packages/*' simply matches nothing. Parsers that require at least one workspace member to parse the root will falsely skip the root manifest.
